content.text:
## Done

Implemented both quick UI fixes in `next-app/components/remote-shell.tsx`:

1. Updated sidebar item styling so `Channels`, `Machines`, and `User` rows have **no default background** and only show a **grey active background** when selected.
2. Removed the “API key is hidden for security” block and now always show the reconnect command in a code-style block (with placeholder values when a key isn’t available yet).

## Validation

- Ran `npm run lint` (passes; 1 pre-existing warning in `components/component-example.tsx`).
- Ran `npm run build` (passes).

content.text:
## Fixed

No, you don’t need to share shadcn docs. I’ve now fixed both in `next-app/components/remote-shell.tsx`:

1. **Sidebar background behavior** is corrected: non-selected `channels/machines/user` items stay transparent, selected item gets grey background.
2. **Reconnect command now shows a real command**: when you open a machine, it auto-generates a fresh API key and displays the full command (not placeholders).

## Note

Generating the real command rotates that machine key once, so any old daemon command for that machine will stop working and should be replaced with the newly shown one.

## Validation

- `npm run lint` passed (with one unrelated pre-existing warning in `components/component-example.tsx`).
- `npm run build` passed.

## Done — both issues fixed

You were right; no shadcn docs needed.

- Replaced the entire **“Run a command”** content with just the selected channel display (`#channel-name`).
- Fixed the grey background bug at the root in `components/ui/sidebar.tsx`: active styles now apply only when `data-active="true"` (not for false/default), so only clicked/active items are grey.

## Validation

- `npm run lint` passes (same pre-existing unrelated warning in `component-example.tsx`).
- `npm run build` passes.

content.content: TODO List Updated

content.text:
## Yep — fixed

Cause was `router.replace()` on machine click, which triggered App Router navigation/remount, so the UI flashed blank.  
I changed machine selection to update URL via `window.history.replaceState(...)` instead, so selecting machines no longer navigates/remounts the page.

## Validation

- `npm run lint` passed (same pre-existing unrelated warning).
- `npm run build` passed.

content.content: TODO List Updated

content.text:
## Done

- Removed the top offline alert (`“My Machine is offline” + Reconnect`) completely.
- Moved **Reconnect** next to **Delete** in the machine header actions.

## What “Reconnect” does

It **re-initializes the browser ↔ relay session** for that selected machine (it does not restart your machine).  
Useful when websocket/relay state gets stale or disconnected.

## Validation

- `npm run lint` passed (only the same pre-existing warning in `component-example.tsx`).
- `npm run build` passed.
also how are we detcing runtimes?

content.content: No matches found

content.text:
## Runtime detection

Right now it’s detected in the **daemon**, not in the UI.

- In `machine-daemon/main.go`, `detectRuntimes()` checks if CLI binaries exist on PATH using `exec.LookPath(...)`.
- Current candidates are hardcoded to:
  - `claude`
  - `codex`
- That detected list is sent during bootstrap/heartbeat (`/api/machines/bootstrap`, `/api/machines/heartbeat`) and then shown in the UI.
